title: Typo in the login page button label
body: The 'Sigin In' button on the login page has a typo. It should read 'Sign In'. This is a cosmetic issue and does not affect functionality. Noticed on Chrome and Firefox.
comments: Confirmed on mobile too.
Low priority but looks unprofessional.

title: User profile photo upload fails silently
body: When a user tries to upload a profile photo larger than 2MB, the upload silently fails with no error message shown. The user is left on the upload screen with no feedback. Affects all browsers. Workaround: resize photo before upload.
comments: Multiple users reported this in support.
Workaround is to compress the image first.

title: Payment gateway returns 500 on checkout
body: Users are unable to complete checkout. The payment gateway integration throws a 500 Internal Server Error when the user clicks 'Pay Now'. This affects 100% of transactions. Revenue is completely blocked. Started after yesterday's deployment.
comments: Confirmed in production. All payments failing.
Rolled back the deployment but issue persists.
Engineering lead escalated to P0.

title: Search results pagination breaks after page 3
body: When navigating search results, pages 1 and 2 load correctly. However, clicking 'Next' from page 3 onwards returns an empty result set even though more results exist. The URL updates correctly with ?page=4 but no results are shown.
comments: Reproducible on staging and production.
Affects all search queries with more than 30 results.

title: Dark mode toggle resets on page refresh
body: The dark mode preference is not persisted across page refreshes. When a user enables dark mode and refreshes the page, the UI reverts to light mode. The preference is not being saved to localStorage or user settings.
comments: Users have complained about this repeatedly.
Should be saved in localStorage at minimum.

title: SQL injection possible in user search endpoint
body: The /api/users/search endpoint does not sanitize the 'query' parameter. Passing a crafted string like ' OR 1=1 -- allows full database table dump. Tested on staging. No authentication bypass yet but data exposure is confirmed. This needs immediate patching.
comments: CVE filing in progress.
Do not disclose publicly until patched.
Affects all environments.

title: CI pipeline fails on every merge to main
body: Since the Dockerfile base image was updated to python:3.12-slim, the CI pipeline fails at the 'install dependencies' step. The error is: 'No module named pip'. All merges to main are blocked. Feature branches are unaffected.
comments: Happens consistently on every PR merge.
Pinning the base image to 3.11-slim fixes it temporarily.
Need a permanent fix.

title: JWT tokens not expiring — sessions last forever
body: Access tokens issued by the auth service do not expire. The 'exp' field in the JWT payload is set to year 9999. This means a stolen token can be used indefinitely. There is currently no token revocation mechanism.
comments: Discovered during internal security audit.
Tokens should expire in 15 minutes with refresh token rotation.
No active exploit detected yet.

title: Dashboard chart renders blank on Safari 16
body: The analytics dashboard chart component does not render on Safari 16. The chart area shows as completely blank. Chrome and Firefox work fine. The console shows: 'ResizeObserver is not defined'. Affects approximately 18% of our user base.
comments: Reproduced on macOS Ventura + Safari 16.
Polyfill for ResizeObserver may fix this.
18% of users is significant.

title: Memory leak in background job worker causes OOM crashes
body: The background job worker process leaks memory over time. After approximately 6 hours of uptime, memory usage reaches 100% and the process is killed by OOM. This causes all queued jobs to fail. Happens in production every night.
comments: Monitoring shows linear memory growth.
Temporary fix: restart worker every 4 hours via cron.
Root cause suspected in the Redis connection pool not releasing.

title: Race condition in order processing causes duplicate charges
body: Under high load, the order processing service occasionally charges customers twice for the same order. Root cause appears to be a race condition: two worker threads both read the order status as 'pending' before either writes 'processing', resulting in duplicate payment API calls. Affects roughly 0.3% of orders during peak traffic.
comments: Customers are reporting double charges.
Finance team has processed 47 manual refunds this week.
Needs idempotency key on payment API calls.
Database-level locking or distributed lock needed.

title: API keys exposed in frontend JavaScript bundle
body: During a routine bundle analysis, a third-party API key for our analytics provider was found hardcoded in the compiled JavaScript bundle served to all users. The key has read/write access to the analytics dashboard. Bundle is publicly accessible at /static/main.js.
comments: Key is currently live and accessible.
Should be rotated immediately.
Must be moved to backend env variables.
Frontend should call our own backend proxy instead.

title: Kubernetes pods crash-looping after config map update
body: After updating the application ConfigMap in Kubernetes, all pods entered a crash-loop backoff state. Logs show: 'KeyError: DATABASE_URL not found in environment'. The ConfigMap update accidentally removed the DATABASE_URL key. Production is down. All services dependent on the database are unavailable.
comments: Production down for 23 minutes.
Rolling back ConfigMap restored service.
Need a validation step before ConfigMap updates go live.
Also need to add DATABASE_URL to the required keys checklist.

title: XSS vulnerability in user-generated comment fields
body: User-submitted comments are rendered as raw HTML in the feed. An attacker can inject a <script> tag that executes arbitrary JavaScript in other users' browsers. Proof of concept: submitting a comment with <script>alert(document.cookie)</script> shows session cookies. Session hijacking is possible.
comments: PoC confirmed on production.
Session cookies are being exposed.
Need to sanitize all user input before rendering.
DOMPurify or similar library recommended.

title: Database connection pool exhausted under moderate load
body: Under moderate load (>200 concurrent users), the application starts throwing 'connection pool exhausted' errors. The current pool size is set to 5 connections. Each API request opens a new connection and some are not being released properly — connection leak suspected in the report generation module which runs long queries.
comments: Pool size of 5 is way too low for production.
Report generation queries take 30-60 seconds each.
Need connection timeout and proper release in finally blocks.
Consider async connection pooling with asyncpg.
